item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Supplementary information: While Pixabay has adapted Pixabay License since January of 2019, they also has clarified that any images uploaded before the new license went into effect remains under CC0. Please see here for details.

The aforementioned image was uploaded on 2012/03/01, so it's safe to use on SCP Wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://pixabay.com/photos/dreidels-hanukkah-spinning-tops-20347/

Now with license compliant imagery. This is a Jewish SCP now.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Belatedly giving my blessing as the author of 2305. Happy to see the wikiwalk project is still going strong!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

The output of one of the tops seen here — specifically 1721-3 — is very similar to to another anomaly, SCP-2305, which can be crosslinked easily.

Editing this:

Since coming into Foundation hands, the object has produced a stream of entirely unhelpful containment procedures for Foundation-held anomalous objects both real and imaginary broken up with periods of persuasively presented but ultimately disastrous advice for various specific researchers and agents coming into contact with the instance.

To this:

Since coming into Foundation hands, the object has produced a stream of entirely unhelpful containment procedures for Foundation-held anomalous objects both real and imaginary broken up with periods of persuasively presented but ultimately disastrous advice for various specific researchers and agents coming into contact with the instance.

If anyone has any other idea, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't fell well versed enough in old-timey Russian literature to make it myself, but there could at least be a sample of the text from "The Bureaucrat's Wife".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1721-3 has two output modes, both unhelpful. It alternates irregularly between providing nonsensical advice, and instructions which seem excellent but prove disastrous if followed. The advice is always pertinent to the occupation of the spinner, though the SCP identifies

should probably be object or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I has an idea similar to this, but this is much more clever. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They spin (revolve) and transcribe aspects of culture, sort of…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah.. The name just… doesn't… make… sense…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehem, that second upvote will have been from me. Excellent concept, perhaps I was expecting something more along the lines of "Someone was using this to write an anomalous novel" but none the less, a decently written article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From the name "Cultural Revolution", I thought at first this would be about Dreidels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted at the description of Instance 3's advice. I like the whole thing, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's up with the rewrites? I remember seeing the Top and the Brick quite some time ago.

Of course +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good stuff. Nice attention to detail, especially in the notes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it then, and hey it is still one of the coolest things on the site. +2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked it then, like it now.

Just…don't leave us again, mmk?
